title: tulotis flava
parts: Tulotis flava has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and flowers, which possess various therapeutic properties. The roots of the Tulotis flava plant contain compounds like alkaloids and glycosides, which are known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The leaves, on the other hand, contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The flowers of the plant contain terpenoids and saponins, which are known to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
side_effects: Tulotis flava can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingestion of the plant's toxic compounds can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain, while prolonged exposure to its volatile oils may lead to headaches and dizziness. Additionally, improper use of tulotis flava may trigger respiratory issues, including asthma-like symptoms and shortness of breath. In severe cases, incorrect consumption or handling of the plant can even lead to organ damage and other systemic complications.
